ontology.foodex2_description: Apple juice drink (diluted, sweetened apple juice beverage)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW is the closest defensible match for diluted sweetened apple juice drink; while not a perfect match for all commercial formulations, it represents the category well.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for generic apple juice drink beverage category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (one glass), providing about 93 kcal and 23 g of carbohydrates including approximately 20 g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Apple juice drink — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Apple juice drink (cr_id D03070002). All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — apple juice
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 36 sourced from University of Sydney GI Database for apple juice (unsweetened, reconstituted from concentrate). This is used as the closest defensible analogous estimate for this sweetened apple juice drink. The glycemic load per 250 ml serving is calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (36 × 22.9) / 100 ≈ 8.2; the input record states 3.3 which corresponds to a smaller reference portion; retained as provided from HPB source with note of discrepancy. GL of 3.3 corresponds to a 100 ml reference portion: (36 × 9.15) / 100 = 3.3.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (7.84 g/100 ml) exceeds the 5 g/100 ml beverage sugar gate; added dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines regardless of low sodium (7 mg/100 ml).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened juice drink; choose plain water, unsweetened sparkling water, or whole fruit instead, as the 7.84 g/100 ml sugar load independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk even at low sodium levels.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened liquid beverage with 7.84 g sugar/100 ml and only 0.11 g dietary fibre/100 ml; liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly without fibre modulation, producing significant postprandial glucose excursions.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this apple juice drink entirely; the absence of meaningful dietary fibre means the 7.84 g/100 ml sugar load is absorbed rapidly, causing significant blood glucose spikes, and whole fresh apple should be substituted if apple flavour is desired.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular event risk; this drink provides 7.84 g sugar/100 ml with negligible protective nutrients.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or eliminate sugar-sweetened juice drinks such as this product, as habitual intake contributes to elevated triglycerides and metabolic syndrome components that increase CVD risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High liquid sugar (7.84 g/100 ml) with negligible fibre and no anti-inflammatory components; added sugar in liquid form promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this sugar-sweetened juice drink, as the high liquid sugar content promotes systemic inflammation without any offsetting anti-inflammatory nutrients such as omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or meaningful dietary fibre.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Apple juice drink is a commercially produced sweetened beverage made from apple juice concentrate reconstituted with water and supplemented with added sugars or sweeteners. It is distinct from pure apple juice in that it contains a lower proportion of actual fruit juice and relies on added sweeteners to achieve its characteristic sweet, mild apple flavour. The drink is clear to pale yellow in colour, with a light, refreshing taste and no significant texture. It is widely consumed as a convenience beverage, particularly as a children's drink or a snack-time refreshment across Singapore and globally. Per 100 ml, it provides 37 kcal, 9.15 g carbohydrates (of which 7.84 g are sugars), 0.1 g protein, 0.02 g fat, 0.11 g dietary fibre, and 7 mg sodium. The high sugar content relative to its fibre content makes it clinically unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 36 (low GI) based on apple juice data from the University of Sydney GI Database; however, the glycemic load per 250 ml serving is approximately 8.2, which is meaningful for blood glucose management. This product is classified as ultra-processed and should be consumed only occasionally, if at all, by individuals managing metabolic or cardiovascular conditions.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D03070002) with confidence 0.95; exact match to the named food item. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database using closest analogous food (apple juice, reconstituted from concentrate) with documented estimation basis.
notes: saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 as this is a fruit-juice-based beverage with negligible fat content; confirmed zero per HPB source (fat_g = 0.02, saturated fat is naturally zero by product formulation). cholesterol_mg is set to 0.0 as this is a plant-derived beverage containing no animal products; naturally zero. calcium_mg is set to 0.0 and iron_mg is set to 0.0 per HPB source which explicitly reports 0.0 for both minerals; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product as of 2026. GI value of 36 is an analogous estimate based on apple juice (reconstituted from concentrate) from the University of Sydney GI Database; the sweetened formulation may result in a marginally higher GI in practice. Glycemic load of 3.3 is calculated per 100 ml reference portion: (36 × 9.15) / 100 = 3.3. This food is classified as a generic beverage with no specific regional cuisine attribution; multilingual local-name fields are not applicable and are set to null. FoodEx2 code A0EJW used as closest defensible match; exact product-level FoodEx2 code for sweetened diluted apple juice drink not confirmed in public FoodEx2 hierarchy — flagged for expert review.
